commands.author: ArthurusDent
commands.command: [bot] thanks for the thorough code analysis. i want to raise a key point that affects your assessment. please review: the "avoid pathological prompt-ingestion latency" justification in commit a8c745a623 is technically incorrect. commit a8c745a623 was made to address issue #62267 (prompt-eval latency). the justification was that auto-setting num_ctx from contextwindow causes "pathological latency." the problem: the auto-fallback never set a variable num_ctx — it always used a static value derived from contextwindow. what actually causes "pathological latency" is a changing num_ctx between requests. ollama explicitly documents that changing num_ctx forces a full model reload (re-allocating the kv cache): https://github.com/ollama/ollama/issues/8903#issuecomment-2642441082 ("anything that changes how model resources are allocated will cause a model reload. size of context (num_ctx)..."). a constant num_ctx is the optimal scenario for this. the "latency" from #62267 is an ollama performance issue (prompt_eval scales quadratically with prompt size on apple silicon), not an openclaw config bug. steipete confirmed this and resolved it as a config/model sizing issue, not a transport or context-window fallback issue. conclusion: the revert of commit c1b9af2770 by a8c745a623 didn't fix #62267 — it created a different, genuine regression (#81878). the most maintainable solution would be an openclaw doctor --fix migration script that maps contextwindow → params.num_ctx for existing configs, preserving the explicit-only intent without breaking working setups. could you please re-evaluate this issue based on this correction to the underlying motivation?
commands.freeform_prompt: [bot] thanks for the thorough code analysis. I want to raise a key point that affects your assessment. Please review: The "avoid pathological prompt-ingestion latency" justification in commit a8c745a623 is technically incorrect. Commit a8c745a623 was made to address Issue #62267 (prompt-eval latency). The justification was that auto-setting num_ctx from contextWindow causes "pathological latency." The problem: the auto-fallback never set a variable num_ctx — it always used a static value derived from contextWindow. What actually causes "pathological latency" is a changing num_ctx between requests. Ollama explicitly documents that changing num_ctx forces a full model reload (re-allocating the KV cache): https://github.com/ollama/ollama/issues/8903#issuecomment-2642441082 ("Anything that changes how model resources are allocated will cause a model reload. Size of context (num_ctx)..."). A constant num_ctx is the optimal scenario for this. The "latency" from #62267 is an Ollama performance issue (prompt_eval scales quadratically with prompt size on Apple Silicon), not an OpenClaw config bug. Steipete confirmed this and resolved it as a config/model sizing issue, not a transport or context-window fallback issue. Conclusion: The revert of commit c1b9af2770 by a8c745a623 didn't fix #62267 — it created a different, genuine regression (#81878). The most maintainable solution would be an openclaw doctor --fix migration script that maps contextWindow → params.num_ctx for existing configs, preserving the explicit-only intent without breaking working setups. Could you please re-evaluate this issue based on this correction to the underlying motivation?

target.title: [Bug]: Ollama `num_ctx` regression
target.author: ArthurusDent
